# UC-22.13.32 · Malware Detection Events on BES Cyber Systems

## Description

We centralize detected-malicious-code events from BES assets so alerts and evidence meet system security management expectations.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes endpoint and sourcetypes WinDefender:Threat, ms:defender:alert. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Fix sourcetype list to your EDR. (2) Map actions to blocked vs quarantined. (3) Create ES notable with CIP-008 link. (4) Retain chain-of-custody fields if exported. Indexes required: `index=endpoint`; Lookups: `bes_cyber_asset_inventory`, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=endpoint` Defender or EDR sourcetypes, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Fix sourcetype list to your EDR. (2) Map actions to blocked vs quarantined. (3) Create ES notable with CIP-008 link. (4) Retain chain-of-custody fields if exported.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Threat OR sourcetype="ms:defender:alert") earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by host, threat_name, action
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Malware Detection Events on BES Cyber Systems (CIP-007-6 R4 Part 4.1)** — We centralize detected-malicious-code events from BES assets so alerts and evidence meet system security management expectations.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=endpoint` Defender or EDR sourcetypes, `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: WinDefender:Threat, ms:defender:alert. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype=WinDefender:Threat, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(bes_system)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, threat_name, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the official BES or cyber-asset list, the ESP edge firewall, and the same Windows or network data this search is built to mirror for that time window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (threats), Time chart (detections), Single value (distinct hosts)

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Threat OR sourcetype="ms:defender:alert") earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by host, threat_name, action
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (threats), Time chart (detections), Single value (distinct hosts)
